title: Butonica acutangula
taxonomy: Butonica acutangula is classified as a plant species within the family Combretaceae. It belongs to the order Myrtales and the family Combretaceae. Butonica acutangula is also known as the pointed caltrop or pointed bindweed. The plant is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and Australia. It is a perennial herb with a sprawling habit and can grow up to 1 meter in height.
uses: Butonica acutangula is also known as Pointed Calotropis, and it has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's extracts are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. In Ayurvedic medicine, Butonica acutangula is used to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems. Its leaves are also used to treat eye infections and skin conditions. Furthermore, it is believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing inflammation and promoting overall health.

side_effects: Butonica acutangula is reported to have several potential side effects, including gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications. It may cause stomach upset, diarrhea, and vomiting in some individuals. The herb may also increase the risk of bleeding in people taking anticoagulant medications. In rare cases, high doses of Butonica acutangula can lead to more severe side effects, such as liver damage and kidney problems.
